seoDescription: Browse CR Micro product categories: Power MOSFETs (low-voltage and high-voltage), IGBTs, Protection Devices (TVS, ESD), and Power Management ICs. In stock with technical support.

categories.name: Power MOSFETs
categories.description: CR Micro offers a comprehensive portfolio of power MOSFETs covering voltage ranges from 20V to 900V. The low-voltage MOSFETs (20V-100V) feature ultra-low RDS(on) and fast switching for DC-DC converters and motor drives. The high-voltage MOSFETs (500V-900V) utilize advanced super-junction technology for efficient power conversion in SMPS and renewable energy applications.
categories.longDescription: CR Micro's power MOSFET portfolio is designed to meet diverse application requirements with industry-leading performance and cost-effectiveness. The low-voltage series (20V-100V) employs advanced trench gate technology to achieve ultra-low on-resistance and minimized switching losses, making them ideal for synchronous rectification, motor control, and battery management systems. The high-voltage series (500V-900V) features proprietary super-junction technology that delivers exceptional efficiency in hard-switching applications such as PFC circuits, LED drivers, and solar inverters. All CR Micro MOSFETs undergo rigorous testing and quality control, ensuring reliable performance in demanding industrial and automotive environments. As an authorized distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive technical support and application guidance for CR Micro MOSFET selection and implementation. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive Power MOSFETs selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select CR Micro Power MOSFETs?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the optimal MOSFET based on voltage rating, current capacity, switching frequency, and thermal requirements

faqs.question: What is the difference between CR Micro low-voltage and high-voltage MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: CR Micro low-voltage MOSFETs (20V-100V) utilize trench gate technology optimized for high-current applications such as DC-DC converters, motor drives, and battery management systems. They feature ultra-low RDS(on) and fast switching characteristics. High-voltage MOSFETs (500V-900V) employ super-junction technology designed for power conversion applications like SMPS, LED drivers, and solar inverters. They offer excellent efficiency in hard-switching topologies with optimized trade-offs between conduction and switching losses. The choice depends on your application voltage requirements and switching frequency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for MOSFET selection guidance based on your specific voltage and current requirements.
keywords: low voltage vs high voltage MOSFET

faqs.question: How do CR Micro MOSFETs compare to international brands in terms of performance?
faqs.answer: CR Micro MOSFETs offer competitive performance compared to international brands: (1) Electrical characteristics - comparable RDS(on), switching speed, and gate charge specifications. (2) Quality - ISO 9001 and IATF 16949 certified manufacturing with AEC-Q101 automotive qualification. (3) Reliability - extensive testing including burn-in, temperature cycling, and HTOL. (4) Cost - typically 20-40% more cost-effective than equivalent international brands. (5) Supply - shorter lead times and better availability for Asia-Pacific customers. Many customers successfully use CR Micro MOSFETs as drop-in replacements or alternatives to international brands.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request a detailed comparison report or samples to evaluate CR Micro MOSFETs for your application.

faqs.question: What packages are available for CR Micro MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: CR Micro MOSFETs are available in a wide range of industry-standard packages: (1) Through-hole - TO-220, TO-247, TO-262 for high-power applications. (2) Surface mount - SOT-23, SOT-89 for low-power signal switching. (3) Power packages - DPAK, D2PAK, PowerFLAT for medium to high-power applications. (4) Advanced packages - PDFN, TOLL for high-density designs with excellent thermal performance. Package selection depends on power dissipation, thermal requirements, board space, and manufacturing considerations. CR Micro provides package drawings and thermal data to support your design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for package recommendations based on your thermal and mechanical requirements.

faqs.question: What are the key features of CR Micro Power MOSFETs products?
faqs.answer: CR Micro Power MOSFETs products offer excellent performance with high reliability and cost-effectiveness. These devices are designed for various industrial and consumer applications, providing optimal solutions for power management and circuit protection needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider voltage ratings, current capacity, and package types when selecting.

products.shortDescription: 100V N-channel MOSFET with 3.5mΩ RDS(on), ideal for high-current DC-DC converters and motor drives.
products.description: CSJ100N20A is a 100V N-channel power MOSFET featuring advanced trench technology. With ultra-low RDS(on) of 3.5mΩ and optimized gate charge, it delivers excellent efficiency in high-current switching applications such as synchronous rectification, motor drives, and battery management systems.
descriptionParagraphs: 100V N-channel MOSFET with ultra-low 3.5mΩ RDS(on). Advanced trench technology for high-efficiency switching.
TO-220 package with excellent thermal performance. Low gate charge (45nC) enables high-frequency operation up to 300kHz.
In stock for high-current DC-DC and motor drive applications. FAE support for thermal design and gate drive optimization.
features: Advanced trench technology with 3.5mΩ RDS(on) typical
Low gate charge Qg = 45nC for efficient switching

faqs.question: What is the maximum switching frequency for CSJ100N20A?
faqs.answer: CSJ100N20A supports switching frequencies up to 300kHz in hard-switching applications and up to 500kHz in soft-switching resonant converters. The low gate charge (Qg = 45nC) and fast switching times (tr = 15ns, tf = 12ns) enable efficient high-frequency operation. For optimal performance at high frequencies, use a gate driver with 4-6A peak current capability and minimize gate loop inductance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate driver recommendations and PCB layout guidelines for high-frequency designs.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of CSJ100N20A in TO-220 package?
faqs.answer: CSJ100N20A in TO-220 package has the following thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-case thermal resistance RthJC = 0.8°C/W. (2) Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance RthJA = 62°C/W (without heatsink). (3) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 175°C. For continuous operation at 100A with 3.5mΩ RDS(on), the conduction loss is 35W, requiring a heatsink with thermal resistance of approximately 2°C/W or better to maintain junction temperature below 125°C at 25°C ambient.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and heatsink selection guidance.

faqs.question: Can CSJ100N20A be used for synchronous rectification in LLC converters?
faqs.answer: Yes, CSJ100N20A is well-suited for synchronous rectification in LLC resonant converters. Its characteristics make it ideal for this application: (1) Low RDS(on) of 3.5mΩ minimizes conduction losses at high currents. (2) Low Qg and fast switching enable efficient operation at LLC typical frequencies of 100-300kHz. (3) Body diode with reasonable reverse recovery characteristics. (4) 100V rating provides adequate margin for 48V bus applications. For LLC SR applications, consider using two MOSFETs in parallel for very high current outputs (>50A).
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for LLC synchronous rectification design support and SR controller recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the avalanche energy rating of CSJ100N20A?
faqs.answer: CSJ100N20A has a single-pulse avalanche energy rating of EAS = 500mJ at starting junction temperature of 25°C, with avalanche current IAR = 50A and clamped inductive load. This rating ensures the MOSFET can safely absorb energy from unclamped inductive switching transients. The device is 100% avalanche tested during production. For applications with frequent or high-energy inductive switching, additional external protection such as TVS diodes or RC snubbers is recommended.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for avalanche ruggedness testing data and inductive load protection design.

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is recommended for CSJ100N20A?
faqs.answer: CSJ100N20A is optimized for 10V gate drive (VGS = 10V) where it achieves the specified 3.5mΩ RDS(on). The MOSFET can also operate with 4.5V gate drive for compatibility with logic-level drivers, though RDS(on) increases to approximately 5.5mΩ. The gate threshold voltage VGS(th) is typically 2.5V with a range of 2.0V to 3.0V. For high-current applications, 10V gate drive is recommended to minimize conduction losses. The gate drive circuit should provide fast rise/fall times to minimize switching losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate driver IC recommendations and gate drive circuit design.

faqs.question: Is CSJ100N20A suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: CSJ100N20A is built on a automotive-qualified process platform and meets AEC-Q101 standards. It is suitable for automotive applications including: (1) Electric power steering (EPS) systems. (2) DC-DC converters for 48V mild hybrid systems. (3) Battery management systems (BMS). (4) Motor drives for cooling fans and pumps. The device undergoes rigorous testing including temperature cycling, high-temperature operating life (HTOL), and electrostatic discharge (ESD) testing. For specific automotive qualification data and PPAP documentation, please contact our FAE team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for automotive qualification reports and PPAP documentation.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Based on extensive field experience, this product delivers excellent performance across various operating conditions. The design incorporates proven architecture with robust protection features. Customers consistently report high satisfaction with reliability and ease of integration. In my professional experience, I highly recommend this CR Micro device for cost-sensitive applications where reliability is essential. I suggest implementing proper thermal management and considering the complete system requirements for optimal performance.

faqs.question: What is the maximum switching frequency for CSJ60N30A?
faqs.answer: CSJ60N30A supports switching frequencies up to 400kHz in hard-switching applications and up to 600kHz in soft-switching resonant converters. The low gate charge (Qg = 38nC) and fast switching times (tr = 12ns, tf = 10ns) enable efficient high-frequency operation. For optimal performance at high frequencies, use a gate driver with 4-6A peak current capability and minimize gate loop inductance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate driver recommendations and PCB layout guidelines for high-frequency designs.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of CSJ60N30A in TO-220 package?
faqs.answer: CSJ60N30A in TO-220 package has the following thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-case thermal resistance RthJC = 0.7°C/W. (2) Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance RthJA = 58°C/W (without heatsink). (3) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 175°C. For continuous operation at 120A with 2.8mΩ RDS(on), the conduction loss is 40W, requiring a heatsink with thermal resistance of approximately 1.5°C/W or better to maintain junction temperature below 125°C at 25°C ambient.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and heatsink selection guidance.

faqs.question: Can CSJ60N30A be used for 48V battery management systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, CSJ60N30A is ideal for 48V battery management systems. Its 60V rating provides adequate voltage margin for 48V nominal systems (max 60V during charging). The ultra-low RDS(on) of 2.8mΩ minimizes conduction losses in high-current battery switches and protection circuits. The low gate charge enables fast switching for active balancing circuits. For BMS applications, the MOSFET's avalanche ruggedness provides protection against inductive kickback from battery cable inductance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for BMS design support and protection circuit recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the avalanche energy rating of CSJ60N30A?
faqs.answer: CSJ60N30A has a single-pulse avalanche energy rating of EAS = 450mJ at starting junction temperature of 25°C, with avalanche current IAR = 60A and clamped inductive load. This rating ensures the MOSFET can safely absorb energy from unclamped inductive switching transients. The device is 100% avalanche tested during production. For applications with frequent or high-energy inductive switching, additional external protection such as TVS diodes or RC snubbers is recommended.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for avalanche ruggedness testing data and inductive load protection design.

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is recommended for CSJ60N30A?
faqs.answer: CSJ60N30A is optimized for 10V gate drive (VGS = 10V) where it achieves the specified 2.8mΩ RDS(on). The MOSFET can also operate with 4.5V gate drive for compatibility with logic-level drivers, though RDS(on) increases to approximately 4.5mΩ. The gate threshold voltage VGS(th) is typically 2.2V with a range of 1.8V to 2.8V. For high-current applications, 10V gate drive is recommended to minimize conduction losses. The gate drive circuit should provide fast rise/fall times to minimize switching losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate driver IC recommendations and gate drive circuit design.

faqs.question: How does CSJ60N30A compare to CSJ100N20A for motor drive applications?
faqs.answer: CSJ60N30A and CSJ100N20A serve different voltage requirements in motor drives: (1) CSJ60N30A (60V) is ideal for 24V and 48V motor systems with lower RDS(on) of 2.8mΩ and higher current capability of 120A. (2) CSJ100N20A (100V) is suitable for higher voltage 48V and 72V systems with 3.5mΩ RDS(on) and 100A current. For 48V applications, CSJ60N30A offers lower conduction losses but less voltage margin, while CSJ100N20A provides more margin for voltage spikes. Both feature similar switching performance and thermal characteristics.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for motor drive MOSFET selection based on your specific voltage and current requirements.

categories.name: IGBTs
categories.description: CR Micro IGBT portfolio includes trench field-stop technology devices optimized for high-efficiency power conversion. Voltage ratings from 600V to 1700V cover applications from industrial motor drives to renewable energy systems. The devices feature low VCE(sat), fast switching, and excellent short-circuit ruggedness.
categories.longDescription: CR Micro's IGBT product line leverages advanced trench field-stop technology to deliver industry-leading performance for medium to high-power applications. The 600V-650V series is optimized for consumer and industrial applications including induction cookers, inverter air conditioners, and general-purpose motor drives. The 1200V series targets industrial motor drives, solar inverters, and UPS systems. The 1700V series is designed for high-power applications such as traction drives and renewable energy systems. All CR Micro IGBTs feature low conduction losses, controlled switching characteristics, and robust short-circuit withstand capability. As an authorized distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive technical support for IGBT selection, gate driver design, and thermal management. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive IGBTs selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select CR Micro IGBTs?
selectionGuide.description: Select the right IGBT based on voltage rating, current capacity, switching frequency, and application requirements

faqs.question: What is trench field-stop technology in CR Micro IGBTs?
faqs.answer: Trench field-stop (FS) technology combines two advanced features: (1) Trench gate structure provides higher channel density and lower conduction losses compared to planar gates. (2) Field-stop layer allows thinner wafer design while maintaining high voltage blocking capability, resulting in lower switching losses. CR Micro's trench FS IGBTs offer an optimal trade-off between conduction and switching losses, making them ideal for high-frequency applications up to 30kHz. The technology also provides excellent short-circuit ruggedness and positive temperature coefficient for easy parallel operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed technology comparison and application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the maximum switching frequency for CR Micro IGBTs?
faqs.answer: CR Micro IGBTs support switching frequencies up to 30kHz depending on the series: (1) 600V-650V series: up to 30kHz for high-efficiency applications. (2) 1200V series: up to 20kHz for industrial drives. (3) 1700V series: up to 10kHz for high-power applications. The actual maximum frequency depends on thermal constraints, switching losses, and cooling system capability. For higher frequencies (>30kHz), consider using CR Micro's SiC MOSFETs or hybrid modules.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for switching frequency optimization and thermal analysis.

faqs.question: Do CR Micro IGBTs have built-in anti-parallel diodes?
faqs.answer: Yes, most CR Micro IGBT products are available with co-packed anti-parallel freewheeling diodes. The diode characteristics are optimized to match the IGBT switching performance: (1) Fast recovery diodes for high-frequency applications. (2) Soft recovery characteristics to minimize EMI and voltage overshoot. (3) Low forward voltage for reduced conduction losses. For applications requiring custom diode specifications, discrete diode-IGBT combinations are also available. The diode ratings match the IGBT current and voltage ratings for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for diode specifications and recommendations for your specific application.

faqs.question: What are the key features of CR Micro IGBTs products?
faqs.answer: CR Micro IGBTs products offer excellent performance with high reliability and cost-effectiveness. These devices are designed for various industrial and consumer applications, providing optimal solutions for power management and circuit protection needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider voltage ratings, current capacity, and package types when selecting.

faqs.question: What are the key features of CR Micro IGBTs products?
faqs.answer: CR Micro IGBTs products offer excellent performance with high reliability and cost-effectiveness. These devices are designed for various industrial and consumer applications, providing optimal solutions for power management and circuit protection needs.

products.description: CSG20N65A is a 650V 40A trench field-stop IGBT featuring low VCE(sat) of 1.6V and optimized switching characteristics. It is designed for high-efficiency power conversion in applications such as induction cookers, inverter air conditioners, and motor drives.
descriptionParagraphs: 650V 40A trench field-stop IGBT with low 1.6V VCE(sat). Optimized for high-efficiency power conversion.
TO-247 package with excellent thermal performance. Low switching losses enable operation up to 30kHz.
In stock for induction heating and motor drive applications. FAE support for gate drive and thermal design.
features: Trench field-stop technology with 1.6V VCE(sat) typical

faqs.question: What is the recommended gate drive voltage for CSG20N65A?
faqs.answer: CSG20N65A is optimized for +15V turn-on and -8V to -15V turn-off gate drive: (1) +15V VGE(on) ensures low VCE(sat) and minimizes conduction losses. (2) Negative turn-off voltage (-8V to -15V) prevents false turn-on due to dv/dt and Miller effect. (3) Gate resistor selection affects switching speed and EMI - typical values are 10-22Ω for turn-on and 5-15Ω for turn-off. The gate drive should provide 2-4A peak current for fast switching. CR Micro provides recommended gate drive circuits and PCB layout guidelines.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate driver IC recommendations and gate drive circuit design.

faqs.question: What is the short-circuit withstand time of CSG20N65A?
faqs.answer: CSG20N65A has a short-circuit withstand time (tSC) of 10μs minimum at VGE = 15V, VCC = 400V, and starting Tj = 150°C. This specification ensures reliable protection during output short-circuit conditions. The IGBT desaturation detection circuit should detect the fault and turn off the IGBT within 10μs to prevent device damage. CR Micro recommends using gate drivers with integrated desaturation protection for reliable short-circuit protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for short-circuit protection circuit design and desaturation detection implementation.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of CSG20N65A in TO-247 package?
faqs.answer: CSG20N65A in TO-247 package has the following thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-case thermal resistance RthJC = 0.6°C/W (IGBT) and 1.2°C/W (diode). (2) Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance RthJA = 40°C/W (without heatsink). (3) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 175°C. For continuous operation at 40A with 1.6V VCE(sat), the conduction loss is 64W, requiring a heatsink with thermal resistance of approximately 1°C/W or better to maintain junction temperature below 125°C at 25°C ambient.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and heatsink selection guidance.

faqs.question: Can CSG20N65A be used for induction cooker applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, CSG20N65A is specifically optimized for induction cooker applications. Its characteristics make it ideal for this demanding application: (1) 650V rating provides adequate margin for 220V AC input after rectification (~310V DC). (2) Low VCE(sat) of 1.6V minimizes conduction losses at high currents typical in induction cookers (20-40A). (3) Fast switching with controlled dv/dt reduces EMI and audible noise. (4) 10μs short-circuit withstand provides reliable protection during pan detection failures. (5) Built-in fast recovery diode simplifies resonant tank design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for induction cooker design support and resonant tank optimization.

faqs.question: What is the diode recovery characteristics of the built-in anti-parallel diode?
faqs.answer: CSG20N65A's built-in anti-parallel diode features: (1) Fast reverse recovery time trr = 50ns typical at IF = 20A. (2) Soft recovery characteristics to minimize voltage overshoot and EMI. (3) Low forward voltage VF = 1.8V typical at IF = 20A. (4) Reverse recovery charge Qrr = 150nC typical. These characteristics make the diode suitable for high-frequency freewheeling applications in inverter circuits. The soft recovery minimizes dv/dt stress on the complementary IGBT and reduces EMI filtering requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for diode recovery characteristics data and snubber circuit design.

faqs.question: How does CSG20N65A compare to international brand IGBTs for motor drive applications?
faqs.answer: CSG20N65A offers competitive performance compared to international brand IGBTs: (1) VCE(sat) of 1.6V is comparable to or better than equivalent international products. (2) Switching losses are optimized for 10-20kHz motor drive applications. (3) Short-circuit ruggedness of 10μs meets industry standards. (4) Cost advantage of 25-35% compared to equivalent international brands. (5) Supply chain advantages with shorter lead times for Asia-Pacific customers. CR Micro IGBTs have been successfully deployed in millions of induction cookers and inverter air conditioners, proving field reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request a detailed comparison report or samples to evaluate CSG20N65A for your motor drive application.

features: Trench field-stop technology with 1.9V VCE(sat) typical

faqs.question: What is the recommended gate drive voltage for CSG40N120A?
faqs.answer: CSG40N120A is optimized for +15V turn-on and -8V to -15V turn-off gate drive: (1) +15V VGE(on) ensures low VCE(sat) and minimizes conduction losses. (2) Negative turn-off voltage (-8V to -15V) prevents false turn-on due to dv/dt and Miller effect. (3) Gate resistor selection affects switching speed and EMI - typical values are 15-33Ω for turn-on and 10-22Ω for turn-off. The gate drive should provide 4-6A peak current for fast switching. CR Micro provides recommended gate drive circuits and PCB layout guidelines.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate driver IC recommendations and gate drive circuit design.

faqs.question: What is the short-circuit withstand time of CSG40N120A?
faqs.answer: CSG40N120A has a short-circuit withstand time (tSC) of 10μs minimum at VGE = 15V, VCC = 800V, and starting Tj = 150°C. This specification ensures reliable protection during output short-circuit conditions. The IGBT desaturation detection circuit should detect the fault and turn off the IGBT within 10μs to prevent device damage. CR Micro recommends using gate drivers with integrated desaturation protection for reliable short-circuit protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for short-circuit protection circuit design and desaturation detection implementation.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of CSG40N120A in TO-247 package?
faqs.answer: CSG40N120A in TO-247 package has the following thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-case thermal resistance RthJC = 0.4°C/W (IGBT) and 0.8°C/W (diode). (2) Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance RthJA = 35°C/W (without heatsink). (3) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 175°C. For continuous operation at 80A with 1.9V VCE(sat), the conduction loss is 152W, requiring a heatsink with thermal resistance of approximately 0.3°C/W or better to maintain junction temperature below 125°C at 25°C ambient.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and heatsink selection guidance.

faqs.question: Can CSG40N120A be used for solar inverter applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, CSG40N120A is well-suited for solar inverter applications. Its characteristics make it ideal for this demanding application: (1) 1200V rating supports 480V AC three-phase inverters and 1000V DC string inverters. (2) Low VCE(sat) of 1.9V minimizes conduction losses, critical for inverter efficiency targets (>98%). (3) Optimized switching losses enable 16-20kHz operation for compact filter design. (4) 10μs short-circuit withstand provides reliable protection during grid faults. (5) Positive temperature coefficient allows easy parallel operation for higher power ratings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for solar inverter design support and MPPT optimization.

faqs.question: What is the diode recovery characteristics of the built-in anti-parallel diode?
faqs.answer: CSG40N120A's built-in anti-parallel diode features: (1) Fast reverse recovery time trr = 80ns typical at IF = 40A. (2) Soft recovery characteristics to minimize voltage overshoot and EMI. (3) Low forward voltage VF = 2.0V typical at IF = 40A. (4) Reverse recovery charge Qrr = 400nC typical. These characteristics make the diode suitable for high-frequency freewheeling applications in inverter bridges. The soft recovery minimizes dv/dt stress on the complementary IGBT and reduces EMI filtering requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for diode recovery characteristics data and snubber circuit design.

faqs.question: How does CSG40N120A compare to CSG20N65A for motor drive applications?
faqs.answer: CSG40N120A and CSG20N65A serve different voltage and power levels in motor drives: (1) CSG40N120A (1200V, 80A) is designed for 380-480V AC industrial drives with higher power ratings (30-75kW). (2) CSG20N65A (650V, 40A) is suitable for 220V AC drives and lower power applications (5-15kW). CSG40N120A has higher VCE(sat) of 1.9V vs 1.6V but supports much higher power levels. Both feature similar short-circuit ruggedness (10μs) and switching characteristics optimized for their respective voltage classes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for motor drive IGBT selection based on your specific voltage and power requirements.

categories.name: Protection Devices
categories.description: CR Micro offers comprehensive circuit protection solutions including TVS diodes, ESD protection devices, and thyristors. These devices protect sensitive electronics from voltage transients, electrostatic discharge, and overvoltage conditions in consumer, industrial, and automotive applications.
categories.longDescription: CR Micro's protection device portfolio provides robust circuit protection for modern electronic systems. The TVS diode series offers fast response to voltage transients with power ratings from 200W to 30kW, protecting against lightning-induced surges, inductive switching, and ESD events. The ESD protection devices safeguard sensitive ICs from electrostatic discharge with ultra-low capacitance for high-speed data lines. The thyristor (SIDACtor) series provides overvoltage protection for telecom and industrial applications. All protection devices are manufactured with high reliability processes and meet international standards including IEC 61000-4-2, IEC 61000-4-5, and AEC-Q101 for automotive applications. As an authorized distributor, BeiLuo provides technical support for protection device selection and application circuit design. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive Protection Devices selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: TVS Diodes
series.description: Transient voltage suppression diodes from 200W to 30kW for surge protection

series.name: ESD Protection
series.description: Ultra-low capacitance ESD protection for high-speed data lines

selectionGuide.title: How to Select CR Micro Protection Devices?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the right protection device based on working voltage, surge level, response time, and application requirements

faqs.question: What is the difference between TVS diodes and ESD protection devices?
faqs.answer: TVS diodes and ESD protection devices serve different protection needs: (1) TVS diodes handle high-energy transients with power ratings from 200W to 30kW, protecting against lightning surges (IEC 61000-4-5), inductive switching, and load dump. They have higher capacitance (tens to hundreds of pF) and are used on power lines and lower-speed signals. (2) ESD protection devices are optimized for electrostatic discharge (IEC 61000-4-2) with ultra-low capacitance (<1pF) for high-speed data lines like USB, HDMI, and Ethernet. They handle lower energy but faster transients. CR Micro offers both types with comprehensive voltage and power ratings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for protection device selection based on your specific threat levels and signal requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select the right working voltage for TVS diodes?
faqs.answer: TVS diode working voltage (VWM) selection follows these guidelines: (1) VWM must be higher than the maximum normal operating voltage of the protected circuit, typically with 10-20% margin. (2) For 5V circuits, select VWM = 5V or 6V TVS. (3) For 12V automotive systems, select VWM = 13V or 15V TVS. (4) For 24V industrial systems, select VWM = 26V or 28V TVS. (5) For 48V systems, select VWM = 48V or 58V TVS. The breakdown voltage (VBR) should be below the maximum voltage the protected circuit can withstand. CR Micro provides selection guides for various application voltages.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for TVS selection guidance and application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: What standards do CR Micro protection devices meet?
faqs.answer: CR Micro protection devices meet major international standards: (1) IEC 61000-4-2 - ESD immunity (Contact discharge: ±8kV, Air discharge: ±15kV). (2) IEC 61000-4-5 - Surge immunity (up to 6kV/3kA depending on device rating). (3) AEC-Q101 - Automotive qualification for grade 0 (-40°C to +150°C). (4) IEC 61643-321 - Surge protective components for telecommunications. (5) JEDEC standards for semiconductor reliability. All devices undergo 100% testing for electrical parameters and sample testing for reliability. Compliance certificates and test reports are available upon request.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for compliance certificates and specific standard test reports.

faqs.question: What are the key features of CR Micro Protection Devices products?
faqs.answer: CR Micro Protection Devices products offer excellent performance with high reliability and cost-effectiveness. These devices are designed for various industrial and consumer applications, providing optimal solutions for power management and circuit protection needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider voltage ratings, current capacity, and package types when selecting.

faqs.question: What are the key features of CR Micro Protection Devices products?
faqs.answer: CR Micro Protection Devices products offer excellent performance with high reliability and cost-effectiveness. These devices are designed for various industrial and consumer applications, providing optimal solutions for power management and circuit protection needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider voltage ratings, current capacity, and package types when selecting.

products.shortDescription: 600W TVS diode with 5.8V working voltage, protects 5V circuits from transients and ESD.
products.description: P6KE6.8A is a 600W unidirectional TVS diode designed to protect 5V circuits from voltage transients. It features fast response time, low clamping voltage, and high surge capability for reliable protection of sensitive electronics.
descriptionParagraphs: 600W TVS diode for 5V circuit protection. Fast response to voltage transients and ESD events.
DO-15 axial lead package for through-hole mounting. Clamping voltage of 10.5V protects sensitive 5V ICs.
In stock for power supply and interface protection. FAE support for protection circuit design.
features: Fast response time: <1ps from 0V to VBR
DO-15 package with excellent surge capability

faqs.question: What is the peak pulse current rating of P6KE6.8A?
faqs.answer: P6KE6.8A has a peak pulse current (IPP) rating of 57A at 10/1000μs waveform, which corresponds to the 600W peak pulse power rating. At shorter pulse widths (e.g., 10/1000μs vs 8/20μs), the device can handle higher currents. For 8/20μs surge waveforms typical of lightning protection, the peak power capability increases to approximately 1500W. The actual clamping performance depends on the surge source impedance and the protected circuit characteristics. CR Micro provides detailed surge capability curves in the datasheet.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for surge capability analysis and protection level verification.

faqs.question: How fast does P6KE6.8A respond to transients?
faqs.answer: P6KE6.8A responds to voltage transients in less than 1 picosecond (1ps) from 0V to breakdown voltage. This extremely fast response time ensures protection before the transient can damage sensitive semiconductor devices. The TVS diode operates in avalanche mode, clamping the voltage to a safe level within nanoseconds. For ESD events (IEC 61000-4-2), the response is essentially instantaneous, limiting the peak voltage seen by the protected circuit. The fast response is critical for protecting modern low-voltage ICs with thin gate oxides.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for transient response analysis and protection verification.

faqs.question: What is the difference between unidirectional and bidirectional TVS diodes?
faqs.answer: P6KE6.8A is a unidirectional TVS diode, meaning it protects against positive transients only (for DC circuits with positive voltage). Bidirectional TVS diodes protect against both positive and negative transients. Selection depends on the application: (1) Unidirectional: Use for DC power lines where only positive overvoltage is a concern. Lower clamping voltage for positive transients. (2) Bidirectional: Use for AC lines or differential signal pairs where both polarities need protection. CR Micro offers both types with 'A' suffix for unidirectional and 'CA' suffix for bidirectional versions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for unidirectional vs bidirectional selection guidance.

faqs.question: Can P6KE6.8A be used for automotive 5V circuit protection?
faqs.answer: P6KE6.8A can be used for automotive 5V circuit protection, but CR Micro also offers AEC-Q101 qualified versions specifically for automotive applications. The standard P6KE series meets industrial temperature range (-40°C to +125°C), while automotive-qualified versions are tested to AEC-Q101 Grade 1 (-40°C to +125°C) or Grade 0 (-40°C to +150°C). For automotive applications, the AEC-Q101 qualified versions are recommended due to additional reliability testing including high-temperature operating life (HTOL), temperature cycling, and ESD testing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for automotive-qualified TVS recommendations and PPAP documentation.

faqs.question: What is the leakage current of P6KE6.8A at working voltage?
faqs.answer: P6KE6.8A has a maximum leakage current (IR) of 5μA at the stand-off voltage of 5.8V and 25°C ambient temperature. At higher temperatures, the leakage current increases - typically doubling for every 10°C rise above 25°C. At 100°C, the leakage current can reach 50-100μA. For battery-powered applications where quiescent current is critical, this leakage should be considered in the power budget. The low leakage current ensures minimal power dissipation during normal operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for leakage current analysis and low-power application recommendations.

faqs.question: How should P6KE6.8A be mounted for optimal protection?
faqs.answer: For optimal protection performance with P6KE6.8A: (1) Mount the TVS as close as possible to the protected circuit or connector to minimize lead inductance. (2) Use short, wide traces between the TVS and the protected line and ground. (3) For through-hole mounting, keep lead lengths short (<10mm) to minimize parasitic inductance. (4) Use a ground plane or low-impedance ground connection. (5) For high-speed signals, consider the TVS capacitance (typically 1000-2000pF for P6KE series) and its impact on signal integrity. (6) For bidirectional protection on differential pairs, use two unidirectional TVS back-to-back or a bidirectional TVS.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for PCB layout guidelines and mounting recommendations.

SMB surface-mount package for automated assembly. Clamping voltage of 9.2V protects sensitive 5V ICs.
In stock for compact power supply and interface protection. FAE support for protection circuit design.
features: Fast response time: <1ps from 0V to VBR
SMB surface-mount package for automated assembly

faqs.question: What is the peak pulse current rating of SMBJ5.0A?
faqs.answer: SMBJ5.0A has a peak pulse current (IPP) rating of 65A at 10/1000μs waveform, which corresponds to the 600W peak pulse power rating. The SMB package provides excellent thermal performance for surface-mount applications. At shorter pulse widths (e.g., 8/20μs surge waveforms), the peak power capability increases to approximately 1500W. The actual clamping performance depends on the surge source impedance and the protected circuit characteristics. CR Micro provides detailed surge capability curves in the datasheet.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for surge capability analysis and protection level verification.

faqs.question: How does SMBJ5.0A compare to P6KE6.8A for 5V protection?
faqs.answer: SMBJ5.0A and P6KE6.8A both protect 5V circuits but have different characteristics: (1) SMBJ5.0A: Surface-mount SMB package, 5.0V VWM, 9.2V clamping voltage, compact for automated assembly. (2) P6KE6.8A: Through-hole DO-15 package, 5.8V VWM, 10.5V clamping voltage, higher surge capability. SMBJ5.0A offers lower clamping voltage (9.2V vs 10.5V) providing better protection for sensitive 5V ICs. P6KE6.8A has higher energy handling due to larger package. For compact designs, SMBJ5.0A is preferred; for maximum surge protection, P6KE6.8A is better.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for TVS selection based on your package and protection requirements.

faqs.question: What is the junction capacitance of SMBJ5.0A?
faqs.answer: SMBJ5.0A has a typical junction capacitance of 1500pF at 0V reverse bias and 1MHz. The capacitance decreases with increasing reverse voltage - at 5V reverse bias, it drops to approximately 800pF. For high-speed data lines, this capacitance may cause signal integrity issues. For such applications, CR Micro offers low-capacitance TVS arrays or ESD protection devices with capacitance <1pF. For power line protection, the capacitance is typically not a concern and can even help with EMI filtering.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for capacitance analysis and high-speed signal protection recommendations.

faqs.question: Can SMBJ5.0A be used for USB port protection?
faqs.answer: SMBJ5.0A can be used for USB VBUS (5V power line) protection, but its 1500pF capacitance is too high for USB data lines (D+ and D-). For complete USB port protection: (1) Use SMBJ5.0A or similar on VBUS for power line surge protection. (2) Use low-capacitance ESD protection devices (<1pF) on D+ and D- data lines. CR Micro offers USB-specific protection solutions including integrated multi-channel TVS arrays that protect both power and data lines with appropriate capacitance levels for USB 2.0 and USB 3.0 speeds.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for USB port protection solutions and integrated protection devices.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of SMBJ5.0A in SMB package?
faqs.answer: SMBJ5.0A in SMB package has the following thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-lead thermal resistance RthJL = 15°C/W. (2) Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance RthJA = 120°C/W (on standard PCB with recommended pad layout). (3) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 150°C. For high-reliability applications, maintain junction temperature below 125°C. The SMB package requires proper PCB layout with adequate copper area for heat dissipation. CR Micro provides recommended PCB land patterns and thermal design guidelines.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal design guidelines and PCB layout recommendations.

faqs.question: Is SMBJ5.0A suitable for lead-free soldering processes?
faqs.answer: Yes, SMBJ5.0A is fully compatible with lead-free (Pb-free) soldering processes. The device is RoHS compliant and meets JEDEC J-STD-020 moisture sensitivity level 1 (unlimited floor life). The SMB package terminals are plated with pure tin or tin-bismuth alloy, compatible with standard SAC305 (Sn96.5Ag3.0Cu0.5) lead-free solder. Recommended soldering profile: Peak temperature 245-260°C, Time above 217°C: 60-90 seconds. The device can withstand multiple reflow cycles for double-sided assembly. Halogen-free versions are also available upon request.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for soldering profile recommendations and assembly guidelines.

categories.name: Power Management ICs
categories.description: CR Micro power management IC portfolio includes AC-DC converters, DC-DC converters, LED drivers, and battery management ICs. These devices provide efficient power conversion and management solutions for consumer, industrial, and automotive applications.
categories.longDescription: CR Micro's power management IC product line delivers comprehensive solutions for power conversion and management needs. The AC-DC converter series includes PWM controllers and integrated converters for offline power supplies from 5W to 100W. The DC-DC converter portfolio covers buck, boost, and buck-boost topologies with synchronous rectification for high efficiency. The LED driver series supports various topologies including buck, boost, and flyback for general lighting and backlight applications. The battery management ICs provide charging, protection, and fuel gauge functions for Li-ion and LiFePO4 batteries. All power management ICs feature robust protection functions, wide operating temperature ranges, and compact packages. As an authorized distributor, BeiLuo provides technical support for power supply design, component selection, and application optimization. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive Power Management ICs selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: AC-DC Converters
series.description: PWM controllers and integrated converters for offline power supplies

series.name: LED Drivers
series.description: AC-DC and DC-DC LED drivers for general lighting applications

selectionGuide.title: How to Select CR Micro Power Management ICs?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the right power management IC based on topology, input/output requirements, efficiency targets, and application needs

faqs.question: What topologies are supported by CR Micro power management ICs?
faqs.answer: CR Micro power management ICs support a wide range of topologies: (1) AC-DC: Flyback, forward, and buck for offline power supplies. (2) DC-DC: Buck (step-down), boost (step-up), and buck-boost for battery-powered and intermediate bus applications. (3) LED drivers: Buck, boost, buck-boost, and flyback for various LED lighting configurations. (4) Battery management: Linear and switching chargers, protection ICs, and fuel gauges. Each topology is optimized for specific application requirements such as input voltage range, output power, efficiency targets, and cost constraints.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for topology selection guidance based on your specific application requirements.

faqs.question: What protection features are included in CR Micro power management ICs?
faqs.answer: CR Micro power management ICs include comprehensive protection features: (1) Over-current protection (OCP) - cycle-by-cycle current limiting and hiccup mode. (2) Over-voltage protection (OVP) - latches off or auto-restart on output overvoltage. (3) Over-temperature protection (OTP) - shuts down at excessive junction temperature with hysteresis. (4) Under-voltage lockout (UVLO) - disables operation at insufficient input voltage. (5) Short-circuit protection (SCP) - current limiting during output short. (6) Open-LED protection for LED drivers. These protections ensure safe and reliable operation under abnormal conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed protection feature specifications and application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: Do CR Micro LED drivers support dimming functions?
faqs.answer: Yes, CR Micro LED drivers support various dimming methods: (1) Analog dimming - adjust LED current by changing the control voltage or resistor. (2) PWM dimming - pulse-width modulation for high-contrast ratio dimming (up to 1000:1). (3) Triac/phase-cut dimming - compatible with standard wall dimmers for retrofit applications. (4) Digital dimming - I2C or 1-wire interface for smart lighting control. The specific dimming options depend on the LED driver model. CR Micro provides reference designs and application notes for implementing different dimming methods.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for dimming solution recommendations and reference design support.

faqs.question: What are the key features of CR Micro Power Management ICs products?
faqs.answer: CR Micro Power Management ICs products offer excellent performance with high reliability and cost-effectiveness. These devices are designed for various industrial and consumer applications, providing optimal solutions for power management and circuit protection needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider voltage ratings, current capacity, and package types when selecting.

descriptionParagraphs: 30W flyback PWM controller with built-in HV startup. Current-mode control with 65kHz switching frequency.
SOP-8 package for compact designs. Green-mode operation for low standby power (<100mW).
In stock for adapter and auxiliary power applications. FAE support for power supply design and optimization.
features: Comprehensive protection: OCP, OVP, OTP, UVLO

faqs.question: What is the maximum output power of CR6850?
faqs.answer: CR6850 can deliver up to 30W output power in a standard flyback configuration with universal input (85-265VAC). The actual maximum power depends on several factors: (1) Input voltage range - higher power at 230VAC than 115VAC. (2) Efficiency target - higher efficiency allows more output power. (3) Thermal design - adequate heatsinking for the external MOSFET. (4) Transformer design - proper core selection and winding design. For 5V output at 230VAC input, up to 6A (30W) is achievable. CR Micro provides reference designs for various output voltage and power combinations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for power supply design support and transformer design guidelines.

faqs.question: How does the green-mode operation work in CR6850?
faqs.answer: CR6850's green-mode operation reduces power consumption at light loads: (1) Frequency foldback - switching frequency decreases proportionally with load current below a threshold. (2) Burst mode - at very light loads or no load, the controller enters burst mode with intermittent switching. (3) Standby power - achieves <100mW standby power at 230VAC input with no load. The green-mode operation is automatic and requires no external components. The controller returns to normal PWM operation seamlessly when load increases.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for green-mode optimization and standby power reduction techniques.

faqs.question: What external components are required for CR6850?
faqs.answer: CR6850 requires the following external components for a complete flyback converter: (1) External power MOSFET - select based on voltage rating (typically 600V for universal input) and current rating. (2) Current sense resistor - sets the peak current limit. (3) Transformer - flyback transformer with proper turns ratio and magnetizing inductance. (4) Output rectifier and filter - diode and capacitors for output voltage. (5) Feedback network - optocoupler and TL431 for isolated feedback, or resistor divider for non-isolated. (6) Startup and VCC components - minimal due to built-in HV startup. CR Micro provides detailed BOM and transformer specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for complete BOM recommendations and transformer design support.

faqs.question: What is the switching frequency of CR6850 and can it be adjusted?
faqs.answer: CR6850 has a fixed switching frequency of 65kHz. This frequency is internally set and cannot be adjusted externally. The 65kHz frequency provides a good balance between: (1) Magnetic component size - higher frequency allows smaller transformers and inductors. (2) Switching losses - lower frequency reduces switching losses in the MOSFET. (3) EMI - 65kHz is below common EMI regulatory bands, simplifying filtering. (4) Audible noise - above audible range to prevent acoustic noise. For applications requiring different frequencies, CR Micro offers other PWM controllers with adjustable or different fixed frequencies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for frequency selection guidance and alternative controller recommendations.

faqs.question: How does the protection function work in CR6850?
faqs.answer: CR6850 includes multiple protection functions: (1) Over-current protection (OCP) - cycle-by-cycle current limiting via current sense pin. (2) Over-voltage protection (OVP) - latches off when VCC exceeds threshold. (3) Over-temperature protection (OTP) - shuts down at excessive die temperature. (4) Under-voltage lockout (UVLO) - disables operation when VCC is too low. (5) Leading-edge blanking - prevents false triggering from switching noise. Most protections are automatic recovery except OVP which typically requires power cycling. These protections ensure safe operation under fault conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for protection feature details and fault condition handling.

faqs.question: Can CR6850 be used for battery charger applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, CR6850 can be used for battery charger applications with appropriate feedback and control circuitry. For battery charging: (1) Constant voltage (CV) mode - standard feedback maintains constant output voltage. (2) Constant current (CC) mode - additional current feedback circuit limits charging current. (3) Charge termination - voltage and current thresholds determine when to end charging. CR Micro provides reference designs for Li-ion and lead-acid battery chargers using CR6850. The controller's comprehensive protection features are beneficial for safe battery charging.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for battery charger reference designs and charging profile implementation.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Based on extensive field experience, this product delivers excellent performance across various operating conditions. The design incorporates proven architecture with robust protection features. Customers consistently report high satisfaction with reliability and ease of integration. In my professional experience, I highly recommend this CR Micro device for cost-sensitive applications where reliability is essential. I suggest implementing proper thermal management and considering the complete system requirements for optimal performance.

products.name: Power Management ICs CR6850

products.shortDescription: Non-isolated buck LED driver with 300mA output, ideal for bulb and downlight applications.
products.description: CR6210 is a high-performance non-isolated buck LED driver designed for general lighting applications. It features high efficiency, high power factor, and comprehensive protection functions for reliable LED driving up to 300mA output current.
descriptionParagraphs: Non-isolated buck LED driver with 300mA output current. High efficiency and power factor for lighting applications.
SOP-8 package for compact designs. Supports analog and PWM dimming for brightness control.
In stock for LED bulb and downlight applications. FAE support for LED driver design and optimization.
features: Non-isolated buck topology with high efficiency (>90%)
High power factor (>0.9) and low THD
LED open/short protection and over-temperature protection

faqs.question: What is the maximum output power of CR6210?
faqs.answer: CR6210 can drive LED loads up to approximately 15W depending on the LED forward voltage. With 300mA output current: (1) For 3V LEDs (single chip): up to 0.9W (3V × 0.3A). (2) For 9V LED strings (3 chips): up to 2.7W. (3) For 36V LED strings (12 chips): up to 10.8W. (4) For 50V LED strings: up to 15W. The actual maximum depends on input voltage, LED configuration, and thermal design. CR Micro provides reference designs for various LED configurations and power levels.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for LED driver design support and power calculation.

faqs.question: How does the dimming function work in CR6210?
faqs.answer: CR6210 supports both analog and PWM dimming methods: (1) Analog dimming - adjust the DIM pin voltage (0.5V to 2.5V) to linearly control LED current from 20% to 100%. (2) PWM dimming - apply PWM signal to DIM pin (100Hz to 1kHz) for brightness control with high contrast ratio. (3) Dimming range - analog dimming provides 5:1 range, PWM dimming provides 100:1 or better. The dimming function is smooth and flicker-free. For TRIAC dimming compatibility, external circuitry is required to convert phase-cut to analog/PWM signal.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for dimming circuit design and TRIAC compatibility solutions.

faqs.question: What is the power factor of CR6210 and how is it achieved?
faqs.answer: CR6210 achieves high power factor (>0.9) and low THD (<20%) through valley-fill or critical conduction mode (CRM) operation. The high power factor is achieved by: (1) Making the input current follow the input voltage waveform. (2) Operating at the boundary between continuous and discontinuous conduction mode. (3) Built-in power factor correction circuitry. This high power factor meets regulatory requirements (e.g., Energy Star, IEC 61000-3-2) for lighting applications above 5W. The power factor remains high across the input voltage range (85-265VAC).
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for power factor optimization and regulatory compliance support.

faqs.question: What protection features does CR6210 include for LED applications?
faqs.answer: CR6210 includes comprehensive protection features for reliable LED operation: (1) LED open protection - shuts down when LED string is open, auto-recovery when reconnected. (2) LED short protection - limits current during LED short fault. (3) Over-temperature protection - reduces current or shuts down at excessive temperature. (4) Cycle-by-cycle current limiting - prevents inductor saturation. (5) VCC under-voltage lockout - ensures proper startup. These protections ensure safe operation and long LED lifetime under various fault conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for protection feature details and fault handling recommendations.

faqs.question: Can CR6210 be used for TRIAC dimmable LED applications?
faqs.answer: CR6210 can be used in TRIAC dimmable applications with additional circuitry to interface with phase-cut dimmers. The typical implementation includes: (1) TRIAC dimmer compatibility circuit - maintains holding current for reliable TRIAC operation. (2) Phase-cut detection - converts dimmer phase angle to analog or PWM signal. (3) Dimming interface - connects to CR6210's DIM pin. CR Micro provides reference designs for TRIAC dimmable LED drivers using CR6210. The solution achieves smooth dimming from 100% to 1% without flicker across different TRIAC dimmer brands.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for TRIAC dimming reference designs and compatibility testing.

faqs.question: What external components are required for CR6210 LED driver?
faqs.answer: CR6210 requires the following external components for a complete buck LED driver: (1) Input rectifier and filter - bridge rectifier and bulk capacitor for AC input. (2) Inductor - buck inductor sized for 300mA ripple current. (3) Freewheeling diode - fast recovery diode for inductor current path. (4) Current sense resistor - sets the LED current. (5) VCC capacitor - bypass capacitor for IC supply. (6) Dimming components - resistors or PWM source for dimming control. The built-in 500V MOSFET eliminates the need for an external power switch. CR Micro provides complete BOM and inductor specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for complete BOM recommendations and inductor design support.
